Description:
30 DAY ARTEX ELT FUNCTIONAL CHECK- . Functional Check 1) A monthly functional check is recommended to verify operational status of the ELT.
2) Perform this functional check in accordance with SUBTASK 25-62-10-750-01 on page 33.
Notes:
The aircraft transceiver is not recommended for this test, because it is too sensitive to  verify the integrity of the antenna system and it may pick up a weak signal even if the  antenna is disconnected from the ELT. An AM radio provides a better level of  confidence for this test
CAUTION: DO NOT ALLOW THE DURATION OF THIS TEST TO EXCEED 5 SECONDS. THE ELT  WILL TRANSMIT A 406 MHZ SIGNAL AFTER THE ELT IS ACTIVATED FOR  APPROXIMATELY 47 SECONDS. THE COSPAS-SARSAT SATELLITE SYSTEM WILL  CONSIDER THE 406 MHZ TRANSMISSION TO BE A VALID DISTRESS SIGNAL.

Description: 300 HOUR/ 12 MONTH AERONAUTICAL ACCESSORIES CROSSTUBE INSPECTION
Notes: Removal of Replacement Crosstubes from helicopter is not required for inspection. If any damage is noted during the inspection, refer to Section 3.4 Repair or Section 4.0 Troubleshooting Information.   / The indications of corrosion are 1) corrosion deposits (a white or gray colored deposits/powder on aluminum; or amber or brown colored deposits/powder on steel), 2) pits in the aluminum or steel surface, 3) blisters, bulging, or flaking of protective coatings.

Description: 300 HOUR/12 MONTH AERONAUTICAL ACCESSORIES FLIGHTSTEP/FLOATSTEP INSPECTION
300 HOUR/ 12 MONTH AERONAUTICAL ACCESSORIES SKID TUBE INSPECTION
Notes:
Removal of Skid Tubes from the helicopter is not required for  inspection. If any damage is noted during the inspection, refer to  Section 3.1.

Particular care should be taken to inspect in the areas of the  fasteners in the Skid Tubes and all attaching components.

The indications of corrosion are 1) corrosion deposits (a white or  gray powder on aluminum), 2) pits in the aluminum surface, 3)  blisters, bulging, or flaking of protective coatings.
